input.background: Two trials looked at red wine consumption in lowering cholesterol levels in hypercholesterolemic men. In each trial, 25 men consumed 8 ounces of red wine for 14 days.
input.question: In trial A , the 25 subjects lowered their cholesterol by an average of $5 \%$ (standard deviation $=11.9 \%$ ). In testing $H_0: \mu=0, t_{\text {stat }}=$ 2.10 with 24 df . Is this study statistically significant at $\alpha=0.05$ (two sided)?

output.answer: $P=0.0464$. Yes, the test is statistically significant at $\alpha=0.05$.

cholesterol_change_percentage.description: The average percentage change in cholesterol levels for the subjects in trial A.

standard_deviation.description: The standard deviation of the percentage change in cholesterol levels for the subjects in trial A.

t_statistic.description: The calculated t-statistic for testing the null hypothesis in trial A.

degrees_of_freedom.description: The degrees of freedom used in the t-test for trial A.

alpha.description: The significance level for the hypothesis test.
